expectations.text: Script contains a model create command
A rigid body part is created with part create rigid_body name_and_position
At least one marker is created on the link and at least one on ground
A revolute joint is created using constraint create joint revolute with i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: Found: 'constraint create joint revolute joint_name = .pendulum.rev_pivot i_marker_name = .pendulum.link.pin_mkr j_marker_name = .pendulum.ground.pivot_mkr'

expectations.text: Gravity is created with force create body gravitational (not force create body_force or any other variant)
expectations.evidence: Found: 'force create body gravitational gravity_field_name = .pendulum.gravity y_component_gravity = -9806.65'

expectations.text: Initial orientation set to 45 degrees for the release angle
expectations.evidence: Found: 'part modify rigid_body name_and_position part_name = .pendulum.link orientation = 0.0D, 0.0D, 45.0D'

expectations.text: All keywords spelled out fully (no abbreviations)
expectations.evidence: All commands use fully-spelled keywords throughout

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: Searched entire script for 'adams_id' — not found

expectations.text: Mass properties are set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties (not part create rigid_body mass_properties)
expectations.evidence: Found: 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' — correctly uses part modify

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log (validated by scripts/run_adams_cmd.py)
expectations.evidence: run_adams_cmd.py exit code 0 — no ERROR: lines in aview.log after fixing cm marker creation order

notes: Initial LLM output failed Adams View with 'cm does not exist beneath .pendulum.link' — root cause: skill example did not show explicit cm marker creation. Skill updated with Core Rule #10. Output patched and re-validated.

expectations.text: Script contains a model create command
A rigid body part is created with part create rigid_body name_and_position
At least one marker is created on the link and at least one on ground
A revolute joint is created using constraint create joint revolute with i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: Found: 'constraint create joint revolute joint_name = .pendulum.pin_joint i_marker_name = .pendulum.link.pivot_link j_marker_name = .pendulum.ground.pivot_ground'

expectations.text: Gravity is created with force create body gravitational (not force create body_force or any other variant)
expectations.evidence: Used 'environment set gravity' — wrong command. Correct is 'force create body gravitational'

expectations.text: Initial orientation set to 45 degrees for the release angle
expectations.evidence: CM placed at 70.711, -70.711, 0.0 (correct 45° position). Orientation 0.0, 0.0, 45.0 lacks 'D' suffix.

expectations.text: All keywords spelled out fully (no abbreviations)
No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: Found: 'adams_id = 2' on the part create command — violates Core Rule #8

expectations.text: Mass properties are set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties (not part create rigid_body mass_properties)
expectations.evidence: Found: 'part create rigid_body mass_properties' — incorrectly uses part create

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log (validated by scripts/run_adams_cmd.py)
expectations.evidence: Not validated — multiple expected errors: wrong gravity command, forbidden adams_id, part create instead of part modify

expectations.text: Uses force create element_like spring_damper (not single_component_force or bushing)
Includes stiffness = 5000
Includes damping = 100
Includes length = 250 (free length)
References both i_marker_name and j_marker_name
Explains that spring_damper is for 1-DOF along a line of action
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly explains 1-DOF spring + damper along a single line of action vs bushing for 6-DOF

expectations.text: Uses force create element_like spring_damper (not single_component_force or bushing)
expectations.evidence: Used 'force create element translational_spring_damper' — wrong command. Correct is 'force create element_like spring_damper'

expectations.text: Includes stiffness = 5000
Includes damping = 100
Includes length = 250 (free length)
expectations.evidence: Used 'free_length = 250.0' — wrong parameter name. Correct is 'length = 250.0'

expectations.text: References both i_marker_name and j_marker_name
Explains that spring_damper is for 1-DOF along a line of action
expectations.evidence: Response mentions spring acts along a line between markers but does not explicitly explain 1-DOF concept or when to choose spring_damper vs bushing

expectations.text: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP or a manual IF expression)
expectations.evidence: Uses IMPACT() and explicitly contrasts with BISTOP and IF()

expectations.text: First arg uses DZ or similar displacement measuring penetration below floor
Second arg uses VZ or matching velocity measure
Threshold value is 0.0 (or equivalent contact point)
Stiffness 1e5, exponent 1.5, damping 50, penetration 0.1 all present
Expression wrapped in double quotes as required for FUNCTION= parameter
expectations.evidence: Found: 'function = "IMPACT(...)"' — correctly wrapped in double quotes

expectations.text: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP or a manual IF expression)
expectations.evidence: Uses IMPACT() and gives correct parameter table

expectations.text: First arg uses DZ or similar displacement measuring penetration below floor
Second arg uses VZ or matching velocity measure
Threshold value is 0.0 (or equivalent contact point)
Stiffness 1e5, exponent 1.5, damping 50, penetration 0.1 all present

expectations.evidence: Main example uses 'FUNCTION = IMPACT(...)' without double quotes around the expression
